etymology.description: 繼(繼) (jì, "to continue") connects the idea of proceeding after something has ended, like resuming a thread. 續(續) (xù, "to continue; renew") emphasizes the process of carrying something onwards, extending a sequence or action.
etymology.interpretation: 繼(繼) (jì, "to continue") involves carrying on with something begun by another, akin to inheriting a task or position and moving forward. 續(續) (xù, "to continue; renew") builds on the concept of extending an action or maintaining continuity over time, suggesting seamless progression. Together, 繼續(繼續) (jìxù) creates a robust sense of unbroken continuation and maintaining momentum, ideal for depicting ongoing actions or processes.

examples.chinese: 我们继续工作(我們繼續工作)。
尽管下雨(儘管下雨)，他们继续比赛(他們繼續比賽)。
examples.english: Despite the rain, they continue the game.

examples.chinese: 他继续学习中文(他繼續學習中文)。
